title: fix: split legacy and launch top-up quests
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Keeps old top-up quest ids as completed legacy cards so users who earned them still see the old quest.
- Adds new launch-era top-up quest ids gated to paid checkout credits from June 21, 2026.
- Shows the launch cutoff as a compact inline note, `from 21/06/26`, in the quest descriptions.
- Evaluates only the new launch-era ids; legacy top-up cards are catalog-only.
- Covers old/new catalog + reward behavior without asserting exact static quest copy.

Checks:
- `npx biome check --write enter.pollinations.ai/test/quests.test.ts`
- `npm run decrypt-vars && npx vitest run test/quests.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

gist.headline: A tale of two quests
gist.blurb: We've split the top-up quests into legacy and launch-era versions. If you earned the old ones, they'll stay in your trophy case forever, while new top-ups from June 21st onward will trigger the fresh launch quests.
gist.summary: Split top-up quests into legacy and launch-era IDs, preserving completed legacy cards for existing users while gating new top-up quests to checkouts from June 21, 2026.
gist.impact: Users who previously completed top-up quests will retain their legacy cards in the catalog, while new top-ups will correctly trigger the updated launch-era quests.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee organizing a quest board or trophy case, separating old glowing legacy scrolls from shiny new quest cards.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny adventurer's hat, organizing a wooden quest board. On one side are glowing, dusty legacy scrolls, and on the other are shiny new quest cards. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
